capabilities.current_personal_engine_owner: personal crawler runner and Roadmap Phase 7 bounded-run evidence
capabilities.main_evidence: Current main owns the runner entrypoint and source registry inputs.
capabilities.personal_engine_evidence: Personal engine reports preserve bounded runner, retry, source-result, and dry-run evidence.
capabilities.current_product_usage: Not connected to an approved public projection.
capabilities.current_limitations: No approved graph persistence or non-production integrated cohort path.
capabilities.reason: Keep main operational ownership while adopting bounded-run and replay mechanics that are absent or incomplete upstream.
capabilities.phase_l_action: Converge runner entrypoint, deterministic run identity, retry/replay contract, and per-source result output in main.
capabilities.migration_or_compatibility_risk: Do not create production dual-write or replace crawled_notices writers during convergence.
capabilities.acceptance_evidence: Selected cohort replay produces deterministic source results without a production write.

capabilities.main_evidence: notice_sources.source_id is the canonical DB source identifier.
capabilities.personal_engine_evidence: The local resolver proves exact source_key resolution and blocks missing or ambiguous mappings.
capabilities.current_product_usage: Report-backed public exposure already excludes unresolved sources.
capabilities.data_backing: CSV snapshot and local resolver output
capabilities.current_limitations: The target-environment source inventory is not authorized for implementation use yet.
capabilities.reason: The exact resolver is directly compatible with the settled source_id/source_key policy and must be brought into main-owned service code.
capabilities.phase_l_action: Port the resolver contract with source_key snapshot preservation, zero/multiple-match blocks, and no source creation.
capabilities.migration_or_compatibility_risk: Fuzzy match or source_key-as-DB-ID regression is forbidden.
capabilities.acceptance_evidence: Selected cohort resolves only exact source mappings; negative missing and ambiguous cases remain blocked.

capabilities.current_personal_engine_owner: personal adapter configuration and source-specific strategy evidence
capabilities.main_evidence: Main has an adapter module and F-0 adapter-backed read-model evidence.
capabilities.personal_engine_evidence: Personal evidence covers configured selectors, common boards, and source-specific adapter strategies.
capabilities.current_product_usage: Diagnostics and report fixtures expose adapter outcomes, not a live service path.
capabilities.data_backing: adapter fixtures and bounded source reports
capabilities.current_limitations: Source-specific detail mapping remains incomplete for several sources.
capabilities.reason: Main interface remains the integration surface; proven personal strategies are selectively folded in by source.
capabilities.phase_l_action: Define the main-owned adapter contract for list, pagination, detail, body, assets, and evidence diagnostics.
capabilities.migration_or_compatibility_risk: A generic anchor result must not be represented as a verified notice-detail mapping.
capabilities.acceptance_evidence: Every pilot source emits its adapter strategy and explicit unresolved or degraded state.

capabilities.current_main_owner: notice URL consumers and repair scripts
capabilities.current_personal_engine_owner: personal canonical-key, URL-alias, and occurrence planning evidence
capabilities.main_evidence: Main retains notice URLs in crawled_notices and public report rows.
capabilities.personal_engine_evidence: Personal reports preserve canonical URL and alias candidates for replay and reconciliation.
capabilities.current_product_usage: Current public prototype links to original notice URLs only.
capabilities.current_limitations: No graph alias persistence exists in main.
capabilities.canonical_owner_after_l: main canonical URL and alias module
capabilities.reason: The graph identity contract needs tested alias behavior without altering legacy routes.
capabilities.phase_l_action: Port canonical URL normalization and alias comparison as a graph-side contract.
capabilities.migration_or_compatibility_risk: Existing original URLs and numeric scholarship routes remain unchanged.
capabilities.acceptance_evidence: Replay identifies equivalent aliases without creating duplicate notices or public routes.

capabilities.current_main_owner: F-0 report read model and crawled_notices compatibility fields
capabilities.current_personal_engine_owner: normalized crawler graph proposal and local adapter output
capabilities.main_evidence: F-0 maps resolved candidates into a review-compatible shape.
capabilities.personal_engine_evidence: The personal graph direction separates notice, occurrence, target, asset, run, and error evidence.
capabilities.current_product_usage: Public MVP consumes a reduced report representation.
capabilities.current_limitations: No approved graph schema implementation or persistence exists.
capabilities.reason: The graph model supplies canonical provenance while current compatibility consumers remain operational.
capabilities.phase_l_action: Implement the approved additive graph representation behind graph-to-legacy comparison.
capabilities.migration_or_compatibility_risk: Implementation requires the authorized target-schema inventory and additive migration review.
capabilities.acceptance_evidence: One normalized graph record maps deterministically to the legacy-compatible review shape.

capabilities.current_personal_engine_owner: asset, attachment recovery, and quality evidence plans
capabilities.main_evidence: Current review UI and public prototype expose image or attachment metadata when present.
capabilities.personal_engine_evidence: Personal reports distinguish no-assets, missing metadata, and unverified download/content cases.
capabilities.current_product_usage: The public product now labels attachment verification as incomplete.
capabilities.data_backing: legacy image URLs and report asset metadata
capabilities.current_limitations: Downloads, OCR, and attachment-content interpretation are not verified.
capabilities.reason: Metadata is sufficient for the first integration boundary; extracted attachment text remains later additive work.
capabilities.phase_l_action: Port attributable asset metadata and retain explicit download/content verification state.
capabilities.migration_or_compatibility_risk: No asset must be credited to a notice without attributable source/detail evidence.
capabilities.acceptance_evidence: Pilot records show attributable metadata or an explicit unavailable state.

capabilities.main_evidence: Admin review can show captured body text and build a draft from it.
capabilities.personal_engine_evidence: Personal work documents body quality, encoding, selector, and detail-resolution signals.
capabilities.current_product_usage: Body presence is visible in the review queue and detail screen.
capabilities.current_limitations: Generic or short body content cannot be treated as verified detail extraction.
capabilities.reason: Source-aware evidence quality is required before reviewer and public projection parity can be trusted.
capabilities.phase_l_action: Port bounded body extraction and quality diagnostics for the pilot sources only.
capabilities.migration_or_compatibility_risk: No external LLM provider call or persisted LLM suggestion is added.
capabilities.acceptance_evidence: Each pilot item records captured, insufficient, blocked, or unavailable body evidence.

capabilities.current_main_owner: Post-Phase E reports and admin crawler diagnostics
capabilities.current_personal_engine_owner: bounded source-health, retry, replay, rollback, and run evidence
capabilities.main_evidence: The diagnostics page exposes report-backed batch, quality, and rollback context.
capabilities.personal_engine_evidence: Personal reports preserve bounded runner outcomes and source-health queues.
capabilities.current_product_usage: Admin diagnostics are explicitly report-backed and read-only.
capabilities.current_limitations: No live monitoring or graph run persistence is active.
capabilities.reason: The existing diagnostics surface can consume integrated run evidence without claiming live monitoring prematurely.
capabilities.phase_l_action: Converge per-source run status, retry, replay, warning, and rollback identifiers into main diagnostics.
capabilities.migration_or_compatibility_risk: Zero-match and disappearance remain diagnostic states, not deletion proof.
capabilities.acceptance_evidence: A pilot replay exposes run, source result, warning, and rollback evidence in the admin path.

capabilities.current_personal_engine_owner: append-only review-event design and adapter review diagnostics
capabilities.main_evidence: Review actions are DB-backed; K prevents a closed item from being re-promoted through the detail route.
capabilities.personal_engine_evidence: J preserves the append-only review event and effective-decision projection design.
capabilities.current_product_usage: Admin screens now state that the current lifecycle is compatibility behavior.
capabilities.data_backing: DB-backed legacy rows plus report diagnostics
capabilities.current_limitations: Immutable review decisions and reviewer parity are not implemented.
capabilities.canonical_owner_after_l: main review event and compatibility projection boundary
capabilities.reason: Legacy review remains active while L introduces an auditable decision model behind a compatibility projection.
capabilities.phase_l_action: Implement append-only events and effective decision projection only after target inventory authorization.
capabilities.migration_or_compatibility_risk: crawled_notices must remain readable and writable until approved cutover; no production dual-write by default.
capabilities.acceptance_evidence: Non-production replay shows reviewer parity, event history, and legacy comparison without duplicate promotion.

capabilities.current_main_owner: scholarships numeric routes and G report-backed public MVP
capabilities.current_personal_engine_owner: graph-to-review/public projection design and reconciliation evidence
capabilities.main_evidence: Numeric scholarship IDs/routes are existing compatibility behavior; G is a fail-closed report prototype.
capabilities.personal_engine_evidence: Personal plans cover projection mapping, reconciliation, and rollback identifiers.
capabilities.current_product_usage: K labels G as a report-backed prototype rather than live DB projection.
capabilities.data_backing: scholarships DB domain and F-0/F-1 JSON report MVP
capabilities.current_limitations: The two public paths are not yet reconciled through a controlled projection.
capabilities.canonical_owner_after_l: main controlled public projection with route compatibility adapter
capabilities.reason: L must preserve existing public IDs while replacing the report-only boundary only after parity proof.
capabilities.phase_l_action: Build a controlled non-production projection preview and public ID/route reconciliation path.
capabilities.migration_or_compatibility_risk: Fail-closed exposure is mandatory; report MVP retirement requires approved projection parity.
capabilities.acceptance_evidence: Selected cohort preview reconciles public IDs/routes with no leakage from blocked or unresolved states.
